title: Basic Science
description: Question batch for the Physics Fundamentals domain.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.explanation: Force is measured in newtons.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of energy?
questions.explanation: Energy is measured in joules.

questions.question: What does speed describe?
questions.explanation: Speed measures how fast distance is covered.

questions.question: Which force pulls objects toward Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity attracts objects toward Earth.

questions.question: What is mass?
answers: Amount of matter in an object
questions.explanation: Mass measures the amount of matter.

questions.question: What is acceleration?
questions.explanation: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity.

questions.question: What kind of quantity is velocity?
questions.explanation: Velocity includes both speed and direction.

questions.question: Which simple machine is a wheel on an axle?
questions.explanation: A wheel and axle is a basic simple machine.

questions.question: What does friction usually do?
questions.explanation: Friction resists motion between surfaces.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of power?
questions.explanation: Power is measured in watts.

questions.question: Which law says force equals mass times acceleration?
questions.explanation: Newton's second law is F = ma.

questions.question: What is inertia?
questions.explanation: Inertia is resistance to a change in motion.

questions.question: What is momentum?
questions.explanation: Momentum equals mass times velocity.

questions.question: Which form of energy does a moving object have?
questions.explanation: Motion gives an object kinetic energy.

questions.question: Which form of energy is stored by position?
questions.explanation: Potential energy is energy stored by position or state.

questions.question: What is voltage?
questions.explanation: Voltage is electric potential difference.

questions.question: What does electric current measure?
questions.explanation: Current is the flow of electric charge.

questions.question: What does resistance do in a circuit?
questions.explanation: Resistance limits current flow.

questions.question: Which wave can travel through a vacuum?
questions.explanation: Light is an electromagnetic wave and can travel in vacuum.

questions.question: What is frequency?
answers: Number of wave cycles per second
questions.explanation: Frequency is how many cycles happen each second.

questions.question: Why does a seatbelt help in a crash?
questions.explanation: A seatbelt restrains the body as inertia carries it forward.

questions.question: Why is work done in physics?
answers: A force moves an object through distance
questions.explanation: Work happens when force causes displacement.

questions.question: Why do objects float in water sometimes?
questions.explanation: Floating happens when buoyant force matches weight.

questions.question: What does conservation of energy mean?
answers: Energy changes form but is not destroyed
questions.explanation: Energy can transform but total energy is conserved.

questions.question: Why does pressure increase underwater?
questions.explanation: Deeper water means more fluid weight overhead.

questions.question: Why are metals good conductors?
questions.explanation: Metals conduct well because electrons move freely.

questions.question: What causes an echo?
questions.explanation: An echo is reflected sound.

questions.question: Why does a prism separate white light?
answers: It creates new colors from heat
questions.explanation: Colors bend by different amounts in a prism.

questions.question: What happens to kinetic energy as speed rises?
questions.explanation: Higher speed means greater kinetic energy.

questions.question: Why do astronauts feel weightless in orbit?
answers: They are in continuous free fall
questions.explanation: Orbiting astronauts are constantly falling around Earth.
